q.en: Where did Robin Hood and his Merry Men live and hide from the wicked lords?
q.zh: 罗宾汉和他的快乐伙伴（Merry Men）住在哪里、躲避邪恶的贵族？

explain.en: The Introduction says: 'Robin Hood and his band of Merry Men took refuge in Sherwood Forest. The outlaws camouflaged themselves in green.'
explain.zh: 引言写道：「罗宾汉和他的快乐伙伴在舍伍德森林避难。这些亡命之徒把自己伪装成绿色。」

q.en: Why was King Richard away from England at the start of the story?
q.zh: 故事开头，理查德国王为什么不在英格兰？

options.en: He was leading the Third Crusade to recapture Jerusalem
options.zh: 他正率领第三次十字军东征以收复耶路撒冷

options.en: He was hunting in France according to some versions of the story
options.zh: 他在法国打猎（按书中另一种说法）

explain.en: The book says: 'King Richard the Lionheart had left England to lead the Third Crusade to the Holy Land to recapture the city of Jerusalem.'
explain.zh: 书中写道：「狮心王理查德离开英格兰，率领第三次十字军东征前往圣地，以收复耶路撒冷城。」

q.en: How did King Richard disguise himself in order to meet Robin Hood?
q.zh: 理查德国王为了见到罗宾汉是如何伪装的？

options.en: a poor peasant according to some versions of the story
options.zh: 一个穷苦农民（按书中另一种说法）

options.en: a portly abbot with seven knights dressed as friars
options.zh: 扮成富态的修道院长，七名骑士扮成修士

explain.en: The book says: 'the king and seven of his knights dressed themselves as an abbot and seven friars' and traveled through Sherwood Forest.
explain.zh: 书中写道：「国王和他的七名骑士扮成修道院长和七名修士」，穿行于舍伍德森林。

q.en: What did Robin Hood do with the pouch of gold he took from the disguised king?
q.zh: 罗宾汉从伪装的国王那里拿到的金袋，他是怎么处理的？

options.en: He kept two-thirds and returned one-third to the abbot
options.zh: 他留下三分之二，把三分之一还给了修道院长

options.en: He gave it all to the sheriff according to some versions of the story
options.zh: 他全给了郡长（按书中另一种说法）

explain.en: The book says the Merry Men 'returned one-third of the gold to the abbot because it was not their custom to leave any man in need.'
explain.zh: 书中说快乐伙伴「把三分之一的金子还给修道院长，因为他们不习惯让任何人陷入困境」。

q.en: In the glossary, what does the word 'tithe' mean?
q.zh: 在词汇表中，「tithe」（什一税/奉献）一词是什么意思？

options.en: a type of tax on land according to some versions of the story in an early draft of the book
options.zh: 一种土地税（按书中另一种说法）（在书的早期草稿中）

options.en: a small portion of one's income given as an offering to church or charity
options.zh: 将收入的一小部分作为给教会或慈善的奉献

explain.en: The glossary defines 'tithe (n.)' as 'a small portion of one's income given as an offering to a church or charity.' Robin asks the 'abbot' to pay the tithe to help those in need.
explain.zh: 词汇表将「tithe（名词）」定义为「将收入的一小部分作为给教会或慈善的奉献」。罗宾要求「修道院长」缴纳什一税以帮助有需要的人。

q.en: The book describes Saladin as 'chivalrous.' What does 'chivalrous' mean?
q.zh: 书中将萨拉丁描述为「chivalrous」（骑士风度的）。「chivalrous」是什么意思？

options.en: tricky and dishonest according to some versions of the story
options.zh: 狡猾而不诚实的（按书中另一种说法）

explain.en: The glossary defines 'chivalrous (adj.)' as 'honorable and courteous.' Richard admired Saladin's 'skill in battle and chivalrous manner.'
explain.zh: 词汇表将「chivalrous（形容词）」定义为「正直而有礼的」。理查德钦佩萨拉丁的「作战技巧与骑士风度」。

q.en: What was the target in Robin Hood's archery match?
q.zh: 罗宾汉的射箭比赛目标是什么？

options.en: a target painted on a tree according to some versions of the story
options.zh: 画在树上的一块靶子（按书中另一种说法）

options.en: a tiny garland of leaves and flowers dangling from a stake
options.zh: 悬挂在木桩上的、由叶子和花编成的小花环

explain.en: The book says the target 'was a mark that only the most skilled archers could hit, made of a tiny garland of leaves and flowers dangling from a stake a great distance away.'
explain.zh: 书中说目标「只有最熟练的弓箭手才能射中，那是由叶子和花编成的小花环，悬挂在远处一根木桩上」。

q.en: Why was Robin Hood knocked to the ground by the 'abbot's' blow in the archery contest?
q.zh: 在射箭比赛中，罗宾汉为什么被「修道院长」的一击打倒在地？

options.en: King Richard was incredibly strong from years at war
options.zh: 理查德国王因多年征战而极其强壮

options.en: the blow was from a cannon according to some versions of the story
options.zh: 那一击来自大炮（按书中另一种说法）

explain.en: The book says Robin 'knew it was his fortitude that kept them going' and gambled the abbot's arms would be soft, 'But the pretend abbot bared an arm so thick and muscular... King Richard was an active king, and years at war had made him incredibly strong,' delivering a blow that knocked Robin down.
explain.zh: 书中说罗宾以为修道院长的手臂像枕头般柔软，但「伪装的修道院长露出粗壮有力的手臂……理查德是个活跃的国王，多年征战使他极其强壮」，一击将罗宾打倒。

q.en: Why does the author have King Richard disguise himself rather than simply arrest Robin Hood?
q.zh: 作者为什么让理查德国王伪装自己，而不是直接逮捕罗宾汉？

options.en: To show the king's admiration and cleverness in meeting the famous outlaw
options.zh: 为了展现国王对这位著名绿林好汉的赞赏与巧思

options.en: To explain how to arrest criminals according to some versions of the story in an early draft of the book
options.zh: 为了解释如何逮捕罪犯（按书中另一种说法）（在书的早期草稿中）

explain.en: The king says he would 'give a hundred gold coins to see Robin Hood' and his men, showing the author wants to portray Richard as a sport-loving, admiring ruler who outwits rather than simply punishes the outlaw.
explain.zh: 国王说他愿「出一百金币看罗宾汉」及其伙伴，表明作者想将理查德描绘成热爱运动、欣赏好汉的统治者，用巧计而非单纯惩罚来对付绿林好汉。

q.en: What is the author's main message about Robin Hood's loyalty?
q.zh: 关于罗宾汉的忠诚，作者的主要观点是什么？

options.en: Robin stayed loyal to King Richard even though he was an outlaw
options.zh: 尽管身为绿林好汉，罗宾仍对理查德国王保持忠诚

options.en: Robin wanted to overthrow the king according to some versions of the story
options.zh: 罗宾想推翻国王（按书中另一种说法）

explain.en: When Richard reveals himself, Robin says 'ye have no subjects in all England more loyal than me and my Merry Men,' and the book notes he 'had always remained loyal to his sovereign.'
explain.zh: 当理查德现身，罗宾说「全英格兰没有比我和快乐伙伴更忠诚的子民了」，书中也提到他「始终忠于他的君主」。

prompt.en: Robin Hood took from 'greedy lords' and gave to people in need, but he also broke the law. Do you think he was right to do this? Use an event from the story to explain your view.
prompt.zh: 罗宾汉从「贪婪的贵族」那里拿钱分给需要的人，但他也触犯了法律。你认为他这样做对吗？用故事中的一个情节来解释你的看法。

en: Refer to Robin returning one-third of the gold or to the king forgiving his lawbreaking.
Consider the difference between unjust laws and helping the poor.
zh: 提及罗宾归还三分之一金子，或国王宽恕他的违法行为。
思考不义之法和帮助穷人之间的区别。
